title: Bulk update issues by query and ID
description:
As a system utility
I want to update multiple issues in one command
So that I can apply consistent changes without repeating manual steps.

Feature: Bulk update issues

Scenario: Update issues selected by filters
  Given a repository with issues of different types and statuses
  When I run "kanbus bulk update --where-type task --where-status open --set-status in_progress --set-assignee "alex""
  Then only matching issues should be updated

Scenario: Update issues selected by explicit IDs
  Given a repository with multiple issues
  When I run "kanbus bulk update --id <issue-1> --id <issue-2> --set-status closed"
  Then only those issues should be updated

Scenario: Bulk update prints a summary of changes
  Given a repository with multiple issues
  When I run "kanbus bulk update --where-status open --set-status in_progress"
  Then stdout should include the number of issues updated
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented bulk update in Rust/Python CLIs with filter+ID selectors and summary output; added BDD coverage in features/issues/issue_bulk_update.feature.
